conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information from you. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 12 years old and I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me gather some details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

First, I need to understand your responsibility for Alex:

**Is Alex living with you, and are you responsible for him (meaning you're caring for him and contributing to his upbringing costs)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home and I'm fully responsible for him. I pay for everything - his food, clothes, school supplies, all that sort of thing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming. Now I need to check a few more things:

**Are you or Alex currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK - we're in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. I need to check a couple more important points:

**Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex (for example, another parent or guardian)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else claims Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital or local authority care for more than 12 weeks?
- In custody/detention?
- Living with someone else (apart from you)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those. Alex is healthy and lives at home with me full-time. He's just a normal 12-year-old going to school!

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent, thank you for all that information. Let me just read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules before giving you my final assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! I've reviewed the official guidance. Based on all the information you've provided, I can now give you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the necessary criteria:

✅ Alex is under 16 (he's 12 years old)  
✅ You both live in the UK  
✅ You are responsible for Alex - he lives with you and you pay for his care and upbringing  
✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him  
✅ There are no special circumstances affecting eligibility  

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit for Alex. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. He is under 16 years old (aged 12), lives with you in the UK (Bristol), and you are responsible for him - you care for him and pay for his food, clothes, school supplies and other costs. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. He is not in hospital, care, or custody for extended periods. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you're usually responsible if you live with them, which you do.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 12-year-old son Alex. All the key criteria are met: Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Alex's care and expenses, no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as hospital stays or care arrangements) that would affect eligibility.

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you're usually responsible if you live with them, which you do.
